response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial/GIS experience through GISCollective and the Green Map platform, and has touched distributed systems handling high throughput at Dunnhumby. However, the role's primary requirement — modern C++ or Rust for systems-level routing/tiling pipelines — is a critical gap: his C++ exposure is a decade old and scoped to a short NI LabWindows stint, Rust is entirely absent, and his career trajectory has been firmly TypeScript/Node.js for the past several years. There is no evidence of routing, navigation, or tile-processing domain work.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS background: built and shipped GISCollective, contributed to Green Map GIS app, and presented at FOSS4G 2019
Distributed systems exposure: worked on a micro-services infrastructure processing millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby, using async I/O and high-performance data stores
Breadth of systems experience across Linux, embedded, and low-latency contexts, including a C++ wave-field-synthesis system for his bachelor thesis
concerns: C++ experience is ~10 years old, narrowly scoped to NI LabWindows tooling, and not indicative of modern systems C++ proficiency; no Rust experience whatsoever
Career arc is firmly in TypeScript/Node.js web and product engineering — not systems or pipeline engineering — making a pivot to routing tile infrastructure a substantial reorientation
No experience with navigation systems, routing data pipelines, or tile-format processing, which are the core domain skills the role requires
